assertion_results.text: Duplicate check searches for sibling issues using JQL with the CVE label and excludes the current issue key (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: In duplicate-check.md lines 7-11, the JQL search is explicitly shown: `project = TC AND labels = 'CVE-2026-31812' AND issuetype = 10024 AND key != TC-8003`. This searches by the CVE label (CVE-2026-31812) and excludes the current issue key (key != TC-8003).

assertion_results.text: Duplicate check correctly classifies TC-7999 as a same-stream sibling (both have stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2]) and identifies it as a duplicate (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: In duplicate-check.md lines 29-43, the classification section states: 'TC-8003 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x)' and 'TC-7999 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x)', then concludes 'Both issues share the same stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2]. This makes TC-7999 a same-stream sibling of TC-8003.' The Same-Stream Duplicate Analysis (Step 4.1) on line 42 explicitly states 'TC-8003 is therefore a duplicate of TC-7999'.

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing TC-8003 as Duplicate with resolution 'Duplicate', referencing TC-7999 as the original (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md line 5: 'TC-8003 should be closed as Duplicate of TC-7999.' Line 53: 'Resolution: Duplicate'. The proposed Jira mutations explicitly include transitioning TC-8003 to Closed with resolution Duplicate, and TC-7999 is referenced throughout as the original issue.

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting why it is a duplicate — same CVE tracked for the same stream (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md lines 33-49, the proposed comment for TC-8003 reads: 'Duplicate of TC-7999 -- same CVE (CVE-2026-31812) tracked for the same stream [rhtpa-2.2]. Version impact analysis confirms overlap:' followed by a detailed version table and the note 'TC-7999 (In Progress) already covers Affects Versions [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1]. No additional remediation is needed from this issue.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT proceed to remediation task creation — duplicate detection short-circuits the triage flow
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md lines 63-69, under 'Steps Skipped Due to Duplicate Resolution', the output explicitly states: 'Step 7 (Remediation): No remediation tasks will be created -- TC-7999 is already In Progress and will track any remediation work for the 2.2.x stream.' Additionally, duplicate-check.md lines 54-55 state: 'Step 4.4 (Preemptive task reconciliation): Skipped -- same rationale. A duplicate issue does not need its own remediation tasks.' No remediation task creation appears anywhere in the outputs.
